issues.excerpt: We give thanks
issues.canonical: We give thanks to God always for you all, making mention of you in our prayers;

issues.excerpt: Remembering
issues.canonical: Remembering without ceasing your work of faith, and labour of love, and patience of hope in our Lord Jesus Christ, in the sight of God and our Father;

issues.excerpt: Knowing, brethren
issues.canonical: Knowing, brethren beloved, your election of God.

issues.excerpt: For our gospel
issues.canonical: For our gospel came not unto you in word only, but also in power, and in the Holy Ghost, and in much assurance; as ye know what manner of men we were among you for your sake.

issues.excerpt: And ye became followers of us, and of the Lord
issues.canonical: And ye became followers of us, and of the Lord, having received the word in much affliction, with joy of the Holy Ghost:

issues.excerpt: For from you
issues.canonical: For from you sounded out the word of the Lord not only in Macedonia and Achaia, but also in every place your faith to God-ward is spread abroad; so that we need not to speak any th

issues.excerpt: For they themselves shew of us what manner of entering in we had unto you , and how ye turned to God
issues.canonical: For they themselves shew of us what manner of entering in we had unto you, and how ye turned to God from idols to serve the living and true God;

issues.excerpt: And to wait
issues.canonical: And to wait for his Son from heaven, whom he raised from the dead, even Jesus, which delivered us from the wrath to come.

issues.excerpt: But even after that we had suffered before, and were shamefully entreated
issues.canonical: But even after that we had suffered before, and were shamefully entreated, as ye know, at Philippi, we were bold in our God to speak unto you the gospel of God with much contention

issues.excerpt: But as we were allowed
issues.canonical: But as we were allowed of God to be put in trust with the gospel, even so we speak; not as pleasing men, but God, which trieth our hearts.

issues.excerpt: For neither at any time used
issues.canonical: For neither at any time used we flattering words, as ye know, nor a cloak of covetousness; God is witness:

issues.excerpt: Nor of men sought
issues.canonical: Nor of men sought we glory, neither of you, nor yet of others, when we might have been burdensome, as the apostles of Christ.

issues.excerpt: So being affectionately desirous of you
issues.canonical: So being affectionately desirous of you, we were willing to have imparted unto you, not the gospel of God only, but also our own souls, because ye were dear unto us.

issues.excerpt: For ye remember , brethren
issues.canonical: For ye remember, brethren, our labour and travail: for labouring night and day, because we would not be chargeable unto any of you, we preached unto you the gospel of God.

issues.excerpt: As ye know
issues.canonical: As ye know how we exhorted and comforted and charged every one of you, as a father doth his children,

issues.excerpt: That ye would walk
issues.canonical: That ye would walk worthy of God, who hath called you unto his kingdom and glory.

issues.excerpt: For this cause also thank we God
issues.canonical: For this cause also thank we God without ceasing, because, when ye received the word of God which ye heard of us, ye received it not as the word of men, but as it is in truth, the

issues.excerpt: For ye, brethren
issues.canonical: For ye, brethren, became followers of the churches of God which in Judæa are in Christ Jesus: for ye also have suffered like things of your own countrymen, even as they have of the

issues.excerpt: Who both killed the Lord Jesus, and their own prophets
issues.canonical: Who both killed the Lord Jesus, and their own prophets, and have persecuted us; and they please not God, and are contrary to all men:

issues.excerpt: Forbidding
issues.canonical: Forbidding us to speak to the Gentiles that they might be saved, to fill up their sins alway: for the wrath is come upon them to the uttermost.

issues.excerpt: But we, brethren
issues.canonical: But we, brethren, being taken from you for a short time in presence, not in heart, endeavoured the more abundantly to see your face with great desire.

issues.excerpt: Wherefore we would have come unto you
issues.canonical: Wherefore we would have come unto you, even I Paul, once and again; but Satan hindered us.

issues.excerpt: For ye are our glory
issues.canonical: For ye are our glory and joy.

issues.excerpt: And sent Timotheus
issues.canonical: And sent Timotheus, our brother, and minister of God, and our fellowlabourer in the gospel of Christ, to establish you, and to comfort you concerning your faith:

issues.excerpt: That no man should be moved
issues.canonical: That no man should be moved by these afflictions: for yourselves know that we are appointed thereunto.

issues.excerpt: For verily
issues.canonical: For verily, when we were with you, we told you before that we should suffer tribulation; even as it came to pass, and ye know.

issues.excerpt: For this cause
issues.canonical: For this cause, when I could no longer forbear, I sent to know your faith, lest by some means the tempter have tempted you, and our labour be in vain.

issues.excerpt: But now when Timotheus
issues.canonical: But now when Timotheus came from you unto us, and brought us good tidings of your faith and charity, and that ye have good remembrance of us always, desiring greatly to see us, as

issues.excerpt: Therefore, brethren
issues.canonical: Therefore, brethren, we were comforted over you in all our affliction and distress by your faith:

issues.excerpt: For what thanks
issues.canonical: For what thanks can we render to God again for you, for all the joy wherewith we joy for your sakes before our God;

issues.excerpt: Night and day praying exceedingly that we might see
issues.canonical: Night and day praying exceedingly that we might see your face, and might perfect that which is lacking in your faith?

issues.excerpt: Now God
issues.canonical: Now God himself and our Father, and our Lord Jesus Christ, direct our way unto you.

issues.excerpt: And the Lord
issues.canonical: And the Lord make you to increase and abound in love one toward another, and toward all men, even as we do toward you:

issues.excerpt: To the end
issues.canonical: To the end he may stablish your hearts unblameable in holiness before God, even our Father, at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ with all his saints.

issues.excerpt: For this is the will of God
issues.canonical: For this is the will of God, even your sanctification, that ye should abstain from fornication:

issues.excerpt: That every one
issues.canonical: That every one of you should know how to possess his vessel in sanctification and honour;

issues.excerpt: Not in the lust
issues.canonical: Not in the lust of concupiscence, even as the Gentiles which know not God:

issues.excerpt: For God
issues.canonical: For God hath not called us unto uncleanness, but unto holiness.

issues.excerpt: He therefore that despiseth
issues.canonical: He therefore that despiseth, despiseth not man, but God, who hath also given unto us his holy Spirit.

issues.excerpt: But as touching brotherly
issues.canonical: But as touching brotherly love ye need not that I write unto you: for ye yourselves are taught of God to love one another.

issues.excerpt: And indeed
issues.canonical: And indeed ye do it toward all the brethren which are in all Macedonia: but we beseech you, brethren, that ye increase more and more;

issues.excerpt: And that ye study
issues.canonical: And that ye study to be quiet, and to do your own business, and to work with your own hands, as we commanded you;

issues.excerpt: That ye may walk
issues.canonical: That ye may walk honestly toward them that are without, and that ye may have lack of nothing.

issues.excerpt: But I would not have you
issues.canonical: But I would not have you to be ignorant, brethren, concerning them which are asleep, that ye sorrow not, even as others which have no hope.

issues.excerpt: For if we believe
issues.canonical: For if we believe that Jesus died and rose again, even so them also which sleep in Jesus will God bring with him.

issues.excerpt: For this we say
issues.canonical: For this we say unto you by the word of the Lord, that we which are alive and remain unto the coming of the Lord shall not prevent them which are asleep.

issues.excerpt: For the Lord
issues.canonical: For the Lord himself shall descend from heaven with a shout, with the voice of the archangel, and with the trump of God: and the dead in Christ shall rise first:

issues.excerpt: Then we which are alive
issues.canonical: Then we which are alive and remain shall be caught up together with them in the clouds, to meet the Lord in the air: and so shall we ever be with the Lord.

issues.excerpt: Wherefore comfort
issues.canonical: Wherefore comfort one another with these words.

issues.excerpt: For yourselves
issues.canonical: For yourselves know perfectly that the day of the Lord so cometh as a thief in the night.

issues.excerpt: For when they shall say , Peace and safety ; then sudden destruction cometh
issues.canonical: For when they shall say, Peace and safety; then sudden destruction cometh upon them, as travail upon a woman with child; and they shall not escape.

issues.excerpt: But ye, brethren
issues.canonical: But ye, brethren, are not in darkness, that that day should overtake you as a thief.

issues.excerpt: Ye are all the children
issues.canonical: Ye are all the children of light, and the children of the day: we are not of the night, nor of darkness.

issues.excerpt: Therefore let us not sleep
issues.canonical: Therefore let us not sleep, as do others; but let us watch and be sober.

issues.excerpt: For they that sleep
issues.canonical: For they that sleep sleep in the night; and they that be drunken are drunken in the night.

issues.excerpt: But let us, who are of the day, be sober , putting on the breastplate
issues.canonical: But let us, who are of the day, be sober, putting on the breastplate of faith and love; and for an helmet, the hope of salvation.

issues.excerpt: For God hath not appointed
issues.canonical: For God hath not appointed us to wrath, but to obtain salvation by our Lord Jesus Christ,

issues.excerpt: Who died
issues.canonical: Who died for us, that, whether we wake or sleep, we should live together with him.

issues.excerpt: Wherefore comfort
issues.canonical: Wherefore comfort yourselves together, and edify one another, even as also ye do.

issues.excerpt: And we beseech
issues.canonical: And we beseech you, brethren, to know them which labour among you, and are over you in the Lord, and admonish you;

issues.excerpt: And to esteem
issues.canonical: And to esteem them very highly in love for their work’s sake. And be at peace among yourselves.

issues.excerpt: Now we exhort
issues.canonical: Now we exhort you, brethren, warn them that are unruly, comfort the feebleminded, support the weak, be patient toward all men.

issues.excerpt: See
issues.canonical: See that none render evil for evil unto any man; but ever follow that which is good, both among yourselves, and to all men.

issues.excerpt: Rejoice
issues.canonical: Rejoice evermore.

issues.excerpt: Pray
issues.canonical: Pray without ceasing.

issues.excerpt: In every thing give thanks
issues.canonical: In every thing give thanks: for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus concerning you.

issues.excerpt: Quench
issues.canonical: Quench not the Spirit.

issues.excerpt: Prove
issues.canonical: Prove all things; hold fast that which is good.

issues.excerpt: Abstain from all appearance
issues.canonical: Abstain from all appearance of evil.

issues.excerpt: And the very God
issues.canonical: And the very God of peace sanctify you wholly; and I pray God your whole spirit and soul and body be preserved blameless unto the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.

issues.excerpt: Brethren
issues.canonical: Brethren, pray for us.

issues.excerpt: Greet all the brethren
issues.canonical: Greet all the brethren with an holy kiss.

issues.excerpt: I charge
issues.canonical: I charge you by the Lord that this epistle be read unto all the holy brethren.

issues.excerpt: The grace of our Lord
issues.canonical: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you. Amen.

issues.excerpt: Who was before a blasphemer , and a persecutor , and injurious : but I obtained mercy , because
issues.canonical: Who was before a blasphemer, and a persecutor, and injurious: but I obtained mercy, because I did it ignorantly in unbelief.

issues.excerpt: But if any widow have children or nephews , let them learn first to shew piety at home , and to
issues.canonical: But if any widow have children or nephews, let them learn first to shew piety at home, and to requite their parents: for that is good and acceptable before God.

issues.excerpt: I will therefore that the younger women marry , bear children , guide the house , give none
issues.canonical: I will therefore that the younger women marry, bear children, guide the house, give none occasion to the adversary to speak reproachfully.

issues.excerpt: And there are gathered unto him vain men, the children of Belial , and have strengthened themselves against Rehoboam the son of Solomon , when Rehoboam was young and
issues.canonical: And there are gathered unto him vain men, the children of Belial, and have strengthened themselves against Rehoboam the son of Solomon, when Rehoboam was young and tenderhearted, a

issues.excerpt: Then the king of Israel said, Take ye Micaiah , and carry him back to Amon the governor of
issues.canonical: Then the king of Israel said, Take ye Micaiah, and carry him back to Amon the governor of the city, and to Joash the king’s son;

issues.excerpt: And all the people of the land rejoiced : and the city was quiet , after that they had slain
issues.canonical: And all the people of the land rejoiced: and the city was quiet, after that they had slain Athaliah with the sword.

issues.excerpt: So Uzziah slept with his fathers, and they buried him with his fathers in the field of the burial
issues.canonical: So Uzziah slept with his fathers, and they buried him with his fathers in the field of the burial which belonged to the kings; for they said, He is a leper: and Jotham his son reig

issues.excerpt: And the men which were expressed by name rose up, and took the captives , and with the spoil clothed all that were naked among them, and arrayed them, and shod them, and gave them
issues.canonical: And the men which were expressed by name rose up, and took the captives, and with the spoil clothed all that were naked among them, and arrayed them, and shod them, and gave them t

issues.excerpt: That thine eyes may be open upon this house day and night , upon the place whereof thou hast
issues.canonical: That thine eyes may be open upon this house day and night, upon the place whereof thou hast said that thou wouldest put thy name there; to hearken unto the prayer which thy servant

issues.excerpt: Who comforteth
issues.canonical: Who comforteth us in all our tribulation, that we may be able to comfort them which are in any trouble, by the comfort wherewith we ourselves are comforted of God.

issues.excerpt: For as the sufferings
issues.canonical: For as the sufferings of Christ abound in us, so our consolation also aboundeth by Christ.

issues.excerpt: And whether we be afflicted
issues.canonical: And whether we be afflicted, it is for your consolation and salvation, which is effectual in the enduring of the same sufferings which we also suffer: or whether we be comforted, i

issues.excerpt: And our hope
issues.canonical: And our hope of you is steadfast, knowing, that as ye are partakers of the sufferings, so shall ye be also of the consolation.

issues.excerpt: For we would not, brethren
issues.canonical: For we would not, brethren, have you ignorant of our trouble which came to us in Asia, that we were pressed out of measure, above strength, insomuch that we despaired even of life:

issues.excerpt: But we had the sentence
issues.canonical: But we had the sentence of death in ourselves, that we should not trust in ourselves, but in God which raiseth the dead:

issues.excerpt: Who delivered us from so great
issues.canonical: Who delivered us from so great a death, and doth deliver: in whom we trust that he will yet deliver us;

issues.excerpt: Ye also helping
issues.canonical: Ye also helping together by prayer for us, that for the gift bestowed upon us by the means of many persons thanks may be given by many on our behalf.

issues.excerpt: For our rejoicing
issues.canonical: For our rejoicing is this, the testimony of our conscience, that in simplicity and godly sincerity, not with fleshly wisdom, but by the grace of God, we have had our conversation i

issues.excerpt: For we write
issues.canonical: For we write none other things unto you, than what ye read or acknowledge; and I trust ye shall acknowledge even to the end;

issues.excerpt: As also ye have acknowledged
issues.canonical: As also ye have acknowledged us in part, that we are your rejoicing, even as ye also are ours in the day of the Lord Jesus.

issues.excerpt: And to pass
issues.canonical: And to pass by you into Macedonia, and to come again out of Macedonia unto you, and of you to be brought on my way toward Judæa.

issues.excerpt: When I therefore was thus minded , did I use
issues.canonical: When I therefore was thus minded, did I use lightness? or the things that I purpose, do I purpose according to the flesh, that with me there should be yea yea, and nay nay?

issues.excerpt: For the Son
issues.canonical: For the Son of God, Jesus Christ, who was preached among you by us, even by me and Silvanus and Timotheus, was not yea and nay, but in him was yea.

issues.excerpt: For all the promises
issues.canonical: For all the promises of God in him are yea, and in him Amen, unto the glory of God by us.

issues.excerpt: Now he which stablisheth
issues.canonical: Now he which stablisheth us with you in Christ, and hath anointed us, is God;

issues.excerpt: Moreover I call
issues.canonical: Moreover I call God for a record upon my soul, that to spare you I came not as yet unto Corinth.

issues.excerpt: Not for that we have dominion
issues.canonical: Not for that we have dominion over your faith, but are helpers of your joy: for by faith ye stand.

issues.excerpt: But I beseech
issues.canonical: But I beseech you, that I may not be bold when I am present with that confidence, wherewith I think to be bold against some, which think of us as if we walked according to the fles

issues.excerpt: For though we walk
issues.canonical: For though we walk in the flesh, we do not war after the flesh:

issues.excerpt: (For the weapons
issues.canonical: (For the weapons of our warfare are not carnal, but mighty through God to the pulling down of strong holds;)

issues.excerpt: Casting
issues.canonical: Casting down imaginations, and every high thing that exalteth itself against the knowledge of God, and bringing into captivity every thought to the obedience of Christ;

issues.excerpt: And having in a readiness
issues.canonical: And having in a readiness to revenge all disobedience, when your obedience is fulfilled.

issues.excerpt: Do ye look on things after the outward appearance? If any man trust to himself that he is
issues.canonical: Do ye look on things after the outward appearance? If any man trust to himself that he is Christ’s, let him of himself think this again, that, as he is Christ’s, even so are we Chr

issues.excerpt: For though I should boast somewhat more of our authority
issues.canonical: For though I should boast somewhat more of our authority, which the Lord hath given us for edification, and not for your destruction, I should not be ashamed:

issues.excerpt: That I may not seem
issues.canonical: That I may not seem as if I would terrify you by letters.

issues.excerpt: Let such an one
issues.canonical: Let such an one think this, that, such as we are in word by letters when we are absent, such will we be also in deed when we are present.

issues.excerpt: For we dare
issues.canonical: For we dare not make ourselves of the number, or compare ourselves with some that commend themselves: but they measuring themselves by themselves, and comparing themselves among th

issues.excerpt: For we stretch
issues.canonical: For we stretch not ourselves beyond our measure, as though we reached not unto you: for we are come as far as to you also in preaching the gospel of Christ:

issues.excerpt: Not boasting
issues.canonical: Not boasting of things without our measure, that is, of other men’s labours; but having hope, when your faith is increased, that we shall be enlarged by you according to our rule a

issues.excerpt: To preach
issues.canonical: To preach the gospel in the regions beyond you, and not to boast in another man’s line of things made ready to our hand.

issues.excerpt: But he that glorieth
issues.canonical: But he that glorieth, let him glory in the Lord.

issues.excerpt: For not he that commendeth
issues.canonical: For not he that commendeth himself is approved, but whom the Lord commendeth.

issues.excerpt: For I am jealous over you
issues.canonical: For I am jealous over you with godly jealousy: for I have espoused you to one husband, that I may present you as a chaste virgin to Christ.

issues.excerpt: But I fear
issues.canonical: But I fear, lest by any means, as the serpent beguiled Eve through his subtilty, so your minds should be corrupted from the simplicity that is in Christ.

issues.excerpt: For if he that cometh
issues.canonical: For if he that cometh preacheth another Jesus, whom we have not preached, or if ye receive another spirit, which ye have not received, or another gospel, which ye have not accepted

issues.excerpt: But though
issues.canonical: But though I be rude in speech, yet not in knowledge; but we have been throughly made manifest among you in all things.

issues.excerpt: Have I committed
issues.canonical: Have I committed an offence in abasing myself that ye might be exalted, because I have preached to you the gospel of God freely?

issues.excerpt: And when I was present
issues.canonical: And when I was present with you, and wanted, I was chargeable to no man: for that which was lacking to me the brethren which came from Macedonia supplied: and in all things I have

issues.excerpt: As the truth
issues.canonical: As the truth of Christ is in me, no man shall stop me of this boasting in the regions of Achaia.

issues.excerpt: Wherefore? because I love
issues.canonical: Wherefore? because I love you not? God knoweth.

issues.excerpt: But what I do, that I will do, that I may cut
issues.canonical: But what I do, that I will do, that I may cut off occasion from them which desire occasion; that wherein they glory, they may be found even as we.

issues.excerpt: I say
issues.canonical: I say again, Let no man think me a fool; if otherwise, yet as a fool receive me, that I may boast myself a little.

issues.excerpt: That which I speak
issues.canonical: That which I speak, I speak it not after the Lord, but as it were foolishly, in this confidence of boasting.

issues.excerpt: Seeing that many glory
issues.canonical: Seeing that many glory after the flesh, I will glory also.

issues.excerpt: For ye suffer , if a man bring you
issues.canonical: For ye suffer, if a man bring you into bondage, if a man devour you, if a man take of you, if a man exalt himself, if a man smite you on the face.

issues.excerpt: I speak
issues.canonical: I speak as concerning reproach, as though we had been weak. Howbeit whereinsoever any is bold, (I speak foolishly,) I am bold also.

issues.excerpt: Are they Hebrews
issues.canonical: Are they Hebrews? so am I. Are they Israelites? so am I. Are they the seed of Abraham? so am I.

issues.excerpt: Are they ministers
issues.canonical: Are they ministers of Christ? (I speak as a fool) I am more; in labours more abundant, in stripes above measure, in prisons more frequent, in deaths oft.

issues.excerpt: Of the Jews five times received I forty
issues.canonical: Of the Jews five times received I forty stripes save one.

issues.excerpt: Thrice
issues.canonical: Thrice was I beaten with rods, once was I stoned, thrice I suffered shipwreck, a night and a day I have been in the deep;

issues.excerpt: In weariness
issues.canonical: In weariness and painfulness, in watchings often, in hunger and thirst, in fastings often, in cold and nakedness.

issues.excerpt: Beside those things that are without, that which cometh
issues.canonical: Beside those things that are without, that which cometh upon me daily, the care of all the churches.

issues.excerpt: Who is weak
issues.canonical: Who is weak, and I am not weak? who is offended, and I burn not?

issues.excerpt: If I must needs
issues.canonical: If I must needs glory, I will glory of the things which concern mine infirmities.

issues.excerpt: The God
issues.canonical: The God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, which is blessed for evermore, knoweth that I lie not.

issues.excerpt: In Damascus
issues.canonical: In Damascus the governor under Aretas the king kept the city of the Damascenes with a garrison, desirous to apprehend me:

issues.excerpt: And through a window
issues.canonical: And through a window in a basket was I let down by the wall, and escaped his hands.

issues.excerpt: I knew
issues.canonical: I knew a man in Christ above fourteen years ago, (whether in the body, I cannot tell; or whether out of the body, I cannot tell: God knoweth;) such an one caught up to the third he

issues.excerpt: And I knew
issues.canonical: And I knew such a man, (whether in the body, or out of the body, I cannot tell: God knoweth;)

issues.excerpt: How that he was caught
issues.canonical: How that he was caught up into paradise, and heard unspeakable words, which it is not lawful for a man to utter.

issues.excerpt: Of such an one
issues.canonical: Of such an one will I glory: yet of myself I will not glory, but in mine infirmities.

issues.excerpt: For though
issues.canonical: For though I would desire to glory, I shall not be a fool; for I will say the truth: but now I forbear, lest any man should think of me above that which he seeth me to be, or that

issues.excerpt: And lest
issues.canonical: And lest I should be exalted above measure through the abundance of the revelations, there was given to me a thorn in the flesh, the messenger of Satan to buffet me, lest I should

issues.excerpt: For this thing I besought
issues.canonical: For this thing I besought the Lord thrice, that it might depart from me.

issues.excerpt: And he said unto me, My grace
issues.canonical: And he said unto me, My grace is sufficient for thee: for my strength is made perfect in weakness. Most gladly therefore will I rather glory in my infirmities, that the power of Ch

issues.excerpt: Therefore I take pleasure
issues.canonical: Therefore I take pleasure in infirmities, in reproaches, in necessities, in persecutions, in distresses for Christ’s sake: for when I am weak, then am I strong.

issues.excerpt: I am become
issues.canonical: I am become a fool in glorying; ye have compelled me: for I ought to have been commended of you: for in nothing am I behind the very chiefest apostles, though I be nothing.

issues.excerpt: Truly the signs
issues.canonical: Truly the signs of an apostle were wrought among you in all patience, in signs, and wonders, and mighty deeds.

issues.excerpt: For what is it wherein
issues.canonical: For what is it wherein ye were inferior to other churches, except it be that I myself was not burdensome to you? forgive me this wrong.

issues.excerpt: Behold
issues.canonical: Behold, the third time I am ready to come to you; and I will not be burdensome to you: for I seek not yours, but you: for the children ought not to lay up for the parents, but the

issues.excerpt: And I will very gladly
issues.canonical: And I will very gladly spend and be spent for you; though the more abundantly I love you, the less I be loved.

issues.excerpt: But be it so, I did not burden you
issues.canonical: But be it so, I did not burden you: nevertheless, being crafty, I caught you with guile.

issues.excerpt: Did I make a gain
issues.canonical: Did I make a gain of you by any of them whom I sent unto you?

issues.excerpt: I desired
issues.canonical: I desired Titus, and with him I sent a brother. Did Titus make a gain of you? walked we not in the same spirit? walked we not in the same steps?

issues.excerpt: Again, think
issues.canonical: Again, think ye that we excuse ourselves unto you? we speak before God in Christ: but we do all things, dearly beloved, for your edifying.

issues.excerpt: For I fear
issues.canonical: For I fear, lest, when I come, I shall not find you such as I would, and that I shall be found unto you such as ye would not: lest there be debates, envyings, wraths, strifes, back

issues.excerpt: I told you
issues.canonical: I told you before, and foretell you, as if I were present, the second time; and being absent now I write to them which heretofore have sinned, and to all other, that, if I come aga

issues.excerpt: Since
issues.canonical: Since ye seek a proof of Christ speaking in me, which to you-ward is not weak, but is mighty in you.

issues.excerpt: For though
issues.canonical: For though he was crucified through weakness, yet he liveth by the power of God. For we also are weak in him, but we shall live with him by the power of God toward you.

issues.excerpt: Examine yourselves , whether ye be in the faith; prove your own selves
issues.canonical: Examine yourselves, whether ye be in the faith; prove your own selves. Know ye not your own selves, how that Jesus Christ is in you, except ye be reprobates?

issues.excerpt: But I trust
issues.canonical: But I trust that ye shall know that we are not reprobates.

issues.excerpt: Now I pray
issues.canonical: Now I pray to God that ye do no evil; not that we should appear approved, but that ye should do that which is honest, though we be as reprobates.

issues.excerpt: For we can do nothing
issues.canonical: For we can do nothing against the truth, but for the truth.

issues.excerpt: For we are glad
issues.canonical: For we are glad, when we are weak, and ye are strong: and this also we wish, even your perfection.

issues.excerpt: Therefore I write
issues.canonical: Therefore I write these things being absent, lest being present I should use sharpness, according to the power which the Lord hath given me to edification, and not to destruction.

issues.excerpt: Finally
issues.canonical: Finally, brethren, farewell. Be perfect, be of good comfort, be of one mind, live in peace; and the God of love and peace shall be with you.

issues.excerpt: All the saints
issues.canonical: All the saints salute you.

issues.excerpt: The grace
issues.canonical: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, and the love of God, and the communion of the Holy Ghost, be with you all. Amen.

issues.excerpt: For if I make you
issues.canonical: For if I make you sorry, who is he then that maketh me glad, but the same which is made sorry by me?

issues.excerpt: And I wrote
issues.canonical: And I wrote this same unto you, lest, when I came, I should have sorrow from them of whom I ought to rejoice; having confidence in you all, that my joy is the joy of you all.

issues.excerpt: For out
issues.canonical: For out of much affliction and anguish of heart I wrote unto you with many tears; not that ye should be grieved, but that ye might know the love which I have more abundantly unto y

issues.excerpt: But if any have caused
issues.canonical: But if any have caused grief, he hath not grieved me, but in part: that I may not overcharge you all.

issues.excerpt: So that contrariwise
issues.canonical: So that contrariwise ye ought rather to forgive him, and comfort him, lest perhaps such a one should be swallowed up with overmuch sorrow.

issues.excerpt: Wherefore I beseech
issues.canonical: Wherefore I beseech you that ye would confirm your love toward him.

issues.excerpt: For to this end also did I write
issues.canonical: For to this end also did I write, that I might know the proof of you, whether ye be obedient in all things.

issues.excerpt: Lest
issues.canonical: Lest Satan should get an advantage of us: for we are not ignorant of his devices.

issues.excerpt: Furthermore
issues.canonical: Furthermore, when I came to Troas to preach Christ’s gospel, and a door was opened unto me of the Lord,

issues.excerpt: I had no rest
issues.canonical: I had no rest in my spirit, because I found not Titus my brother: but taking my leave of them, I went from thence into Macedonia.

issues.excerpt: Now thanks
issues.canonical: Now thanks be unto God, which always causeth us to triumph in Christ, and maketh manifest the savour of his knowledge by us in every place.

issues.excerpt: For we are unto God
issues.canonical: For we are unto God a sweet savour of Christ, in them that are saved, and in them that perish:

issues.excerpt: To the one
issues.canonical: To the one we are the savour of death unto death; and to the other the savour of life unto life. And who is sufficient for these things?

issues.excerpt: For we are not as many, which corrupt
issues.canonical: For we are not as many, which corrupt the word of God: but as of sincerity, but as of God, in the sight of God speak we in Christ.

issues.excerpt: Ye are our epistle
issues.canonical: Ye are our epistle written in our hearts, known and read of all men:

issues.excerpt: Not that we are sufficient
issues.canonical: Not that we are sufficient of ourselves to think any thing as of ourselves; but our sufficiency is of God;

issues.excerpt: Who also hath made us able
issues.canonical: Who also hath made us able ministers of the new testament; not of the letter, but of the spirit: for the letter killeth, but the spirit giveth life.

issues.excerpt: But if the ministration of death
issues.canonical: But if the ministration of death, written and engraven in stones, was glorious, so that the children of Israel could not steadfastly behold the face of Moses for the glory of his c

issues.excerpt: For if that which is done
issues.canonical: For if that which is done away was glorious, much more that which remaineth is glorious.

issues.excerpt: And not as Moses
issues.canonical: And not as Moses, which put a veil over his face, that the children of Israel could not steadfastly look to the end of that which is abolished:

issues.excerpt: But their minds
issues.canonical: But their minds were blinded: for until this day remaineth the same veil untaken away in the reading of the old testament; which veil is done away in Christ.

issues.excerpt: But even unto this day, when Moses
issues.canonical: But even unto this day, when Moses is read, the veil is upon their heart.

issues.excerpt: Nevertheless
issues.canonical: Nevertheless when it shall turn to the Lord, the veil shall be taken away.

issues.excerpt: Now the Lord
issues.canonical: Now the Lord is that Spirit: and where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is liberty.

issues.excerpt: But we all, with open face
issues.canonical: But we all, with open face beholding as in a glass the glory of the Lord, are changed into the same image from glory to glory, even as by the Spirit of the Lord.

issues.excerpt: But have renounced
issues.canonical: But have renounced the hidden things of dishonesty, not walking in craftiness, nor handling the word of God deceitfully; but by manifestation of the truth commending ourselves to e

issues.excerpt: But if our gospel
issues.canonical: But if our gospel be hid, it is hid to them that are lost:

issues.excerpt: In whom the god
issues.canonical: In whom the god of this world hath blinded the minds of them which believe not, lest the light of the glorious gospel of Christ, who is the image of God, should shine unto them.

issues.excerpt: For we preach
issues.canonical: For we preach not ourselves, but Christ Jesus the Lord; and ourselves your servants for Jesus’ sake.

issues.excerpt: For God
issues.canonical: For God, who commanded the light to shine out of darkness, hath shined in our hearts, to give the light of the knowledge of the glory of God in the face of Jesus Christ.

issues.excerpt: But we have this treasure
issues.canonical: But we have this treasure in earthen vessels, that the excellency of the power may be of God, and not of us.

issues.excerpt: Persecuted
issues.canonical: Persecuted, but not forsaken; cast down, but not destroyed;

issues.excerpt: Always
issues.canonical: Always bearing about in the body the dying of the Lord Jesus, that the life also of Jesus might be made manifest in our body.

issues.excerpt: For we which live
issues.canonical: For we which live are alway delivered unto death for Jesus’ sake, that the life also of Jesus might be made manifest in our mortal flesh.

issues.excerpt: So then death
issues.canonical: So then death worketh in us, but life in you.

issues.excerpt: We having the same spirit
issues.canonical: We having the same spirit of faith, according as it is written, I believed, and therefore have I spoken; we also believe, and therefore speak;

issues.excerpt: Knowing that he which raised
issues.canonical: Knowing that he which raised up the Lord Jesus shall raise up us also by Jesus, and shall present us with you.

issues.excerpt: For which cause we faint not; but though
issues.canonical: For which cause we faint not; but though our outward man perish, yet the inward man is renewed day by day.

issues.excerpt: For our light affliction , which is but for a moment , worketh for us a far more exceeding
issues.canonical: For our light affliction, which is but for a moment, worketh for us a far more exceeding and eternal weight of glory;

issues.excerpt: While we look
issues.canonical: While we look not at the things which are seen, but at the things which are not seen: for the things which are seen are temporal; but the things which are not seen are eternal.

issues.excerpt: For in this we groan
issues.canonical: For in this we groan, earnestly desiring to be clothed upon with our house which is from heaven:

issues.excerpt: Now he that hath wrought
issues.canonical: Now he that hath wrought us for the selfsame thing is God, who also hath given unto us the earnest of the Spirit.

issues.excerpt: (For we walk
issues.canonical: (For we walk by faith, not by sight:)

issues.excerpt: Wherefore we labour
issues.canonical: Wherefore we labour, that, whether present or absent, we may be accepted of him.

issues.excerpt: For we must all appear
issues.canonical: For we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ; that every one may receive the things done in his body, according to that he hath done, whether it be good or bad.

issues.excerpt: For we commend
issues.canonical: For we commend not ourselves again unto you, but give you occasion to glory on our behalf, that ye may have somewhat to answer them which glory in appearance, and not in heart.

issues.excerpt: Wherefore henceforth
issues.canonical: Wherefore henceforth know we no man after the flesh: yea, though we have known Christ after the flesh, yet now henceforth know we him no more.

issues.excerpt: To wit
issues.canonical: To wit, that God was in Christ, reconciling the world unto himself, not imputing their trespasses unto them; and hath committed unto us the word of reconciliation.

issues.excerpt: Now then we are ambassadors for Christ
issues.canonical: Now then we are ambassadors for Christ, as though God did beseech you by us: we pray you in Christ’s stead, be ye reconciled to God.

issues.excerpt: (For he saith, I have heard
issues.canonical: (For he saith, I have heard thee in a time accepted, and in the day of salvation have I succoured thee: behold, now is the accepted time; behold, now is the day of salvation.)

issues.excerpt: Giving no offence
issues.canonical: Giving no offence in any thing, that the ministry be not blamed:

issues.excerpt: In stripes
issues.canonical: In stripes, in imprisonments, in tumults, in labours, in watchings, in fastings;

issues.excerpt: By pureness
issues.canonical: By pureness, by knowledge, by longsuffering, by kindness, by the Holy Ghost, by love unfeigned,

issues.excerpt: By the word of truth
issues.canonical: By the word of truth, by the power of God, by the armour of righteousness on the right hand and on the left,

issues.excerpt: By honour
issues.canonical: By honour and dishonour, by evil report and good report: as deceivers, and yet true;

issues.excerpt: As unknown
issues.canonical: As unknown, and yet well known; as dying, and, behold, we live; as chastened, and not killed;

issues.excerpt: As sorrowful
issues.canonical: As sorrowful, yet alway rejoicing; as poor, yet making many rich; as having nothing, and yet possessing all things.

issues.excerpt: Ye are not straitened
issues.canonical: Ye are not straitened in us, but ye are straitened in your own bowels.

issues.excerpt: 13 Now for a recompence
Be ye not unequally
issues.canonical: Be ye not unequally yoked together with unbelievers: for what fellowship hath righteousness with unrighteousness? and what communion hath light with darkness?

issues.excerpt: And what concord
issues.canonical: And what concord hath Christ with Belial? or what part hath he that believeth with an infidel?

issues.excerpt: And what agreement hath the temple
issues.canonical: And what agreement hath the temple of God with idols? for ye are the temple of the living God; as God hath said, I will dwell in them, and walk in them; and I will be their God, an

issues.excerpt: Wherefore come out
issues.canonical: Wherefore come out from among them, and be ye separate, saith the Lord, and touch not the unclean thing; and I will receive you,

issues.excerpt: And will be a Father unto you
issues.canonical: And will be a Father unto you, and ye shall be my sons and daughters, saith the Lord Almighty.

issues.excerpt: Receive us; we have wronged
issues.canonical: Receive us; we have wronged no man, we have corrupted no man, we have defrauded no man.

issues.excerpt: I speak
issues.canonical: I speak not this to condemn you: for I have said before, that ye are in our hearts to die and live with you.

issues.excerpt: Great
issues.canonical: Great is my boldness of speech toward you, great is my glorying of you: I am filled with comfort, I am exceeding joyful in all our tribulation.

issues.excerpt: For, when we were come into Macedonia
issues.canonical: For, when we were come into Macedonia, our flesh had no rest, but we were troubled on every side; without were fightings, within were fears.

issues.excerpt: Nevertheless
issues.canonical: Nevertheless God, that comforteth those that are cast down, comforted us by the coming of Titus;

issues.excerpt: And not by his coming only, but by the consolation wherewith
issues.canonical: And not by his coming only, but by the consolation wherewith he was comforted in you, when he told us your earnest desire, your mourning, your fervent mind toward me; so that I rej

issues.excerpt: For though
issues.canonical: For though I made you sorry with a letter, I do not repent, though I did repent: for I perceive that the same epistle hath made you sorry, though it were but for a season.

issues.excerpt: Now I rejoice , not that ye were made sorry
issues.canonical: Now I rejoice, not that ye were made sorry, but that ye sorrowed to repentance: for ye were made sorry after a godly manner, that ye might receive damage by us in nothing.

issues.excerpt: For godly
issues.canonical: For godly sorrow worketh repentance to salvation not to be repented of: but the sorrow of the world worketh death.

issues.excerpt: For behold
issues.canonical: For behold this selfsame thing, that ye sorrowed after a godly sort, what carefulness it wrought in you, yea, what clearing of yourselves, yea, what indignation, yea, what fear, ye

issues.excerpt: Wherefore, though
issues.canonical: Wherefore, though I wrote unto you, I did it not for his cause that had done the wrong, nor for his cause that suffered wrong, but that our care for you in the sight of God might a

issues.excerpt: Therefore we were comforted in your comfort
issues.canonical: Therefore we were comforted in your comfort: yea, and exceedingly the more joyed we for the joy of Titus, because his spirit was refreshed by you all.

issues.excerpt: And his inward
issues.canonical: And his inward affection is more abundant toward you, whilst he remembereth the obedience of you all, how with fear and trembling ye received him.

issues.excerpt: How that in a great
issues.canonical: How that in a great trial of affliction the abundance of their joy and their deep poverty abounded unto the riches of their liberality.

issues.excerpt: Praying
issues.canonical: Praying us with much entreaty that we would receive the gift, and take upon us the fellowship of the ministering to the saints.

issues.excerpt: Insomuch that we desired Titus
issues.canonical: Insomuch that we desired Titus, that as he had begun, so he would also finish in you the same grace also.

issues.excerpt: Therefore, as ye abound
issues.canonical: Therefore, as ye abound in every thing, in faith, and utterance, and knowledge, and in all diligence, and in your love to us, see that ye abound in this grace also.

issues.excerpt: I speak
issues.canonical: I speak not by commandment, but by occasion of the forwardness of others, and to prove the sincerity of your love.

issues.excerpt: For ye know the grace
issues.canonical: For ye know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, that, though he was rich, yet for your sakes he became poor, that ye through his poverty might be rich.

issues.excerpt: 11 Now therefore <span class="cw" data-tgt="pjstjst__inspired_version_18670.798">
For if there be first a willing
issues.canonical: For if there be first a willing mind, it is accepted according to that a man hath, and not according to that he hath not.

issues.excerpt: But by an equality
issues.canonical: But by an equality, that now at this time your abundance may be a supply for their want, that their abundance also may be a supply for your want: that there may be equality:

issues.excerpt: As it is written
issues.canonical: As it is written, He that had gathered much had nothing over; and he that had gathered little had no lack.

issues.excerpt: But thanks
issues.canonical: But thanks be to God, which put the same earnest care into the heart of Titus for you.

issues.excerpt: For indeed
issues.canonical: For indeed he accepted the exhortation; but being more forward, of his own accord he went unto you.

issues.excerpt: And we have sent with him the brother , whose praise
issues.canonical: And we have sent with him the brother, whose praise is in the gospel throughout all the churches;

issues.excerpt: Avoiding
issues.canonical: Avoiding this, that no man should blame us in this abundance which is administered by us:

issues.excerpt: Wherefore shew ye to them, and before the churches
issues.canonical: Wherefore shew ye to them, and before the churches, the proof of your love, and of our boasting on your behalf.

issues.excerpt: For I know
issues.canonical: For I know the forwardness of your mind, for which I boast of you to them of Macedonia, that Achaia was ready a year ago; and your zeal hath provoked very many.

issues.excerpt: Yet have I sent the brethren
issues.canonical: Yet have I sent the brethren, lest our boasting of you should be in vain in this behalf; that, as I said, ye may be ready:

issues.excerpt: Therefore I thought
issues.canonical: Therefore I thought it necessary to exhort the brethren, that they would go before unto you, and make up beforehand your bounty, whereof ye had notice before, that the same might b

issues.excerpt: Every man according
issues.canonical: Every man according as he purposeth in his heart, so let him give; not grudgingly, or of necessity: for God loveth a cheerful giver.

issues.excerpt: And God
issues.canonical: And God is able to make all grace abound toward you; that ye, always having all sufficiency in all things, may abound to every good work:

issues.excerpt: (As it is written , He hath dispersed
issues.canonical: (As it is written, He hath dispersed abroad; he hath given to the poor: his righteousness remaineth for ever.

issues.excerpt: Now he that ministereth
issues.canonical: Now he that ministereth seed to the sower both minister bread for your food, and multiply your seed sown, and increase the fruits of your righteousness;)

issues.excerpt: Being enriched
issues.canonical: Being enriched in every thing to all bountifulness, which causeth through us thanksgiving to God.

issues.excerpt: For the administration of this service
issues.canonical: For the administration of this service not only supplieth the want of the saints, but is abundant also by many thanksgivings unto God;

issues.excerpt: Whiles
issues.canonical: Whiles by the experiment of this ministration they glorify God for your professed subjection unto the gospel of Christ, and for your liberal distribution unto them, and unto all me

issues.excerpt: And by their prayer for you

issues.excerpt: To the saints and faithful brethren in Christ
issues.canonical: To the saints and faithful brethren in Christ which are at Colosse: Grace be unto you, and peace, from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

issues.excerpt: We give thanks to God
issues.canonical: We give thanks to God and the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, praying always for you,

issues.excerpt: Since we heard
issues.canonical: Since we heard of your faith in Christ Jesus, and of the love which ye have to all the saints,

issues.excerpt: For the hope which is laid up for you
issues.canonical: For the hope which is laid up for you in heaven, whereof ye heard before in the word of the truth of the gospel;

issues.excerpt: Which is come unto you
issues.canonical: Which is come unto you, as it is in all the world; and bringeth forth fruit, as it doth also in you, since the day ye heard of it, and knew the grace of God in truth:

issues.excerpt: As ye also learned
issues.canonical: As ye also learned of Epaphras our dear fellowservant, who is for you a faithful minister of Christ;

issues.excerpt: Who also declared
issues.canonical: Who also declared unto us your love in the Spirit.

issues.excerpt: For this cause
issues.canonical: For this cause we also, since the day we heard it, do not cease to pray for you, and to desire that ye might be filled with the knowledge of his will in all wisdom and spiritual un

issues.excerpt: That ye might walk
issues.canonical: That ye might walk worthy of the Lord unto all pleasing, being fruitful in every good work, and increasing in the knowledge of God;

issues.excerpt: Strengthened
issues.canonical: Strengthened with all might, according to his glorious power, unto all patience and longsuffering with joyfulness;

issues.excerpt: Giving
issues.canonical: Giving thanks unto the Father, which hath made us meet to be partakers of the inheritance of the saints in light:

issues.excerpt: Who hath delivered
issues.canonical: Who hath delivered us from the power of darkness, and hath translated us into the kingdom of his dear Son:

issues.excerpt: In whom we have redemption through his blood
issues.canonical: In whom we have redemption through his blood, even the forgiveness of sins:

issues.excerpt: Who is the image
issues.canonical: Who is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn of every creature:

issues.excerpt: For by him were all things created
issues.canonical: For by him were all things created, that are in heaven, and that are in earth, visible and invisible, whether they be thrones, or dominions, or principalities, or powers: all thing

issues.excerpt: And he is the head
issues.canonical: And he is the head of the body, the church: who is the beginning, the firstborn from the dead; that in all things he might have the preeminence.

issues.excerpt: For it pleased
issues.canonical: For it pleased the Father that in him should all fulness dwell;

issues.excerpt: And, having made peace
issues.canonical: And, having made peace through the blood of his cross, by him to reconcile all things unto himself; by him, I say, whether they be things in earth, or things in heaven.

issues.excerpt: And you
issues.canonical: And you, that were sometime alienated and enemies in your mind by wicked works, yet now hath he reconciled

issues.excerpt: In the body
issues.canonical: In the body of his flesh through death, to present you holy and unblameable and unreproveable in his sight:

issues.excerpt: If ye continue
issues.canonical: If ye continue in the faith grounded and settled, and be not moved away from the hope of the gospel, which ye have heard, and which was preached to every creature which is under he

issues.excerpt: Who now rejoice
issues.canonical: Who now rejoice in my sufferings for you, and fill up that which is behind of the afflictions of Christ in my flesh for his body’s sake, which is the church:

issues.excerpt: Whereof
issues.canonical: Whereof I am made a minister, according to the dispensation of God which is given to me for you, to fulfil the word of God;

issues.excerpt: To whom God
issues.canonical: To whom God would make known what is the riches of the glory of this mystery among the Gentiles; which is Christ in you, the hope of glory:

issues.excerpt: Whom we preach
issues.canonical: Whom we preach, warning every man, and teaching every man in all wisdom; that we may present every man perfect in Christ Jesus:

issues.excerpt: Whereunto
issues.canonical: Whereunto I also labour, striving according to his working, which worketh in me mightily.

issues.excerpt: That their hearts
issues.canonical: That their hearts might be comforted, being knit together in love, and unto all riches of the full assurance of understanding, to the acknowledgement of the mystery of God, and of

issues.excerpt: In whom are hid
issues.canonical: In whom are hid all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.

issues.excerpt: For though
issues.canonical: For though I be absent in the flesh, yet am I with you in the spirit, joying and beholding your order, and the steadfastness of your faith in Christ.

issues.excerpt: As ye have therefore received
issues.canonical: As ye have therefore received Christ Jesus the Lord, so walk ye in him:

issues.excerpt: Rooted
issues.canonical: Rooted and built up in him, and stablished in the faith, as ye have been taught, abounding therein with thanksgiving.

issues.excerpt: Beware
issues.canonical: Beware lest any man spoil you through philosophy and vain deceit, after the tradition of men, after the rudiments of the world, and not after Christ.

issues.excerpt: For in him dwelleth
issues.canonical: For in him dwelleth all the fulness of the Godhead bodily.

issues.excerpt: In whom also ye are circumcised with the circumcision made without hands
issues.canonical: In whom also ye are circumcised with the circumcision made without hands, in putting off the body of the sins of the flesh by the circumcision of Christ:

issues.excerpt: Buried
issues.canonical: Buried with him in baptism, wherein also ye are risen with him through the faith of the operation of God, who hath raised him from the dead.

issues.excerpt: And you , being dead
issues.canonical: And you, being dead in your sins and the uncircumcision of your flesh, hath he quickened together with him, having forgiven you all trespasses;

issues.excerpt: Blotting out
issues.canonical: Blotting out the handwriting of ordinances that was against us, which was contrary to us, and took it out of the way, nailing it to his cross;

issues.excerpt: Let no man therefore judge
issues.canonical: Let no man therefore judge you in meat, or in drink, or in respect of an holyday, or of the new moon, or of the sabbath days:

issues.excerpt: Which are a shadow of things to come; but the body
issues.canonical: Which are a shadow of things to come; but the body is of Christ.

issues.excerpt: Let no man beguile
issues.canonical: Let no man beguile you of your reward in a voluntary humility and worshipping of angels, intruding into those things which he hath not seen, vainly puffed up by his fleshly mind,

issues.excerpt: And not holding the Head , from which all the body
issues.canonical: And not holding the Head, from which all the body by joints and bands having nourishment ministered, and knit together, increaseth with the increase of God.

issues.excerpt: Wherefore if ye be dead
issues.canonical: Wherefore if ye be dead with Christ from the rudiments of the world, why, as though living in the world, are ye subject to ordinances,

issues.excerpt: 21 (<span class="cw" data-tgt="pjstjst__inspired_version_18670.570">
Which all are to perish with the using
issues.canonical: Which all are to perish with the using;) after the commandments and doctrines of men?

issues.excerpt: Which things have indeed a shew of wisdom
issues.canonical: Which things have indeed a shew of wisdom in will worship, and humility, and neglecting of the body; not in any honour to the satisfying of the flesh.

issues.excerpt: For ye are dead , and your life
issues.canonical: For ye are dead, and your life is hid with Christ in God.

issues.excerpt: When Christ
issues.canonical: When Christ, who is our life, shall appear, then shall ye also appear with him in glory.

issues.excerpt: Mortify
issues.canonical: Mortify therefore your members which are upon the earth; fornication, uncleanness, inordinate affection, evil concupiscence, and covetousness, which is idolatry:

issues.excerpt: In the which ye also walked
issues.canonical: In the which ye also walked some time, when ye lived in them.

issues.excerpt: But now ye also put off
issues.canonical: But now ye also put off all these; anger, wrath, malice, blasphemy, filthy communication out of your mouth.

issues.excerpt: Lie
issues.canonical: Lie not one to another, seeing that ye have put off the old man with his deeds;

issues.excerpt: And have put on the new
issues.canonical: And have put on the new man, which is renewed in knowledge after the image of him that created him:

issues.excerpt: Where there is neither Greek
issues.canonical: Where there is neither Greek nor Jew, circumcision nor uncircumcision, Barbarian, Scythian, bond nor free: but Christ is all, and in all.

issues.excerpt: Put on therefore, as the elect of God , holy
issues.canonical: Put on therefore, as the elect of God, holy and beloved, bowels of mercies, kindness, humbleness of mind, meekness, longsuffering;

issues.excerpt: Forbearing one
issues.canonical: Forbearing one another, and forgiving one another, if any man have a quarrel against any: even as Christ forgave you, so also do ye.

issues.excerpt: And let the peace
issues.canonical: And let the peace of God rule in your hearts, to the which also ye are called in one body; and be ye thankful.

issues.excerpt: Let the word of Christ dwell in you
issues.canonical: Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly in all wisdom; teaching and admonishing one another in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs, singing with grace in your hearts to the Lor

issues.excerpt: Wives
issues.canonical: Wives, submit yourselves unto your own husbands, as it is fit in the Lord.

issues.excerpt: Husbands
issues.canonical: Husbands, love your wives, and be not bitter against them.

issues.excerpt: Children
issues.canonical: Children, obey your parents in all things: for this is well pleasing unto the Lord.

issues.excerpt: Fathers
issues.canonical: Fathers, provoke not your children to anger, lest they be discouraged.

issues.excerpt: Servants
issues.canonical: Servants, obey in all things your masters according to the flesh; not with eyeservice, as menpleasers; but in singleness of heart, fearing God:

issues.excerpt: Knowing that of the Lord
issues.canonical: Knowing that of the Lord ye shall receive the reward of the inheritance: for ye serve the Lord Christ.

issues.excerpt: Continue
issues.canonical: Continue in prayer, and watch in the same with thanksgiving;

issues.excerpt: Withal praying
issues.canonical: Withal praying also for us, that God would open unto us a door of utterance, to speak the mystery of Christ, for which I am also in bonds:

issues.excerpt: Let your speech
issues.canonical: Let your speech be alway with grace, seasoned with salt, that ye may know how ye ought to answer every man.

issues.excerpt: All my state shall Tychicus
issues.canonical: All my state shall Tychicus declare unto you, who is a beloved brother, and a faithful minister and fellowservant in the Lord:

issues.excerpt: Whom I have sent unto you
issues.canonical: Whom I have sent unto you for the same purpose, that he might know your estate, and comfort your hearts;

issues.excerpt: With Onesimus
issues.canonical: With Onesimus, a faithful and beloved brother, who is one of you. They shall make known unto you all things which are done here.

issues.excerpt: Aristarchus
issues.canonical: Aristarchus my fellowprisoner saluteth you, and Marcus, sister’s son to Barnabas, (touching whom ye received commandments: if he come unto you, receive him;)

issues.excerpt: And Jesus
issues.canonical: And Jesus, which is called Justus, who are of the circumcision. These only are my fellowworkers unto the kingdom of God, which have been a comfort unto me.

issues.excerpt: Epaphras
issues.canonical: Epaphras, who is one of you, a servant of Christ, saluteth you, always labouring fervently for you in prayers, that ye may stand perfect and complete in all the will of God.

issues.excerpt: For I bear
issues.canonical: For I bear him record, that he hath a great zeal for you, and them that are in Laodicea, and them in Hierapolis.

issues.excerpt: Salute
issues.canonical: Salute the brethren which are in Laodicea, and Nymphas, and the church which is in his house.

issues.excerpt: And when this epistle
issues.canonical: And when this epistle is read among you, cause that it be read also in the church of the Laodiceans; and that ye likewise read the epistle from Laodicea.

issues.excerpt: And say to Archippus
issues.canonical: And say to Archippus, Take heed to the ministry which thou hast received in the Lord, that thou fulfil it.

issues.excerpt: The salutation by the hand
issues.canonical: The salutation by the hand of me Paul. Remember my bonds. Grace be with you. Amen.

issues.excerpt: And he shall plant the tabernacles of his palace between the seas in the glorious holy
issues.canonical: And he shall plant the tabernacles of his palace between the seas in the glorious holy mountain; yet he shall come to his end, and none shall help him.

issues.excerpt: The tree grew , and was strong , and the height thereof reached unto heaven, and the sight
issues.canonical: The tree grew, and was strong, and the height thereof reached unto heaven, and the sight thereof to the end of all the earth:

issues.excerpt: I make a decree , That in every dominion of my kingdom men tremble and fear before the God

issues.canonical: As he did to the children of Esau, which dwelt in Seir, when he destroyed the Horims from before them; and they succeeded them, and dwelt in their stead even unto this day:

issues.excerpt: And to make thee high above all nations which he hath made, in praise , and in name, and in
issues.canonical: And to make thee high above all nations which he hath made, in praise, and in name, and in honour; and that thou mayest be an holy people unto the Lord thy God, as he hath spoken.

issues.canonical: In the day of prosperity be joyful, but in the day of adversity consider: God also hath set the one over against the other, to the end that man should find nothing after him.
