issues.excerpt: And I soon go to the place of my rest , which is with my Redeemer ; for I know that in him I shall rest. And I rejoice in the day when my mortal shall put on immortality , and shal
issues.canonical: And I soon go to the place of my rest, which is with my Redeemer; for I know that in him I shall rest. And I rejoice in the day when my mortal shall put on immortality, and shall s

issues.excerpt: According
issues.canonical: According as he hath chosen us in him before the foundation of the world, that we should be holy and without blame before him in love:

issues.excerpt: Having predestinated
issues.canonical: Having predestinated us unto the adoption of children by Jesus Christ to himself, according to the good pleasure of his will,

issues.excerpt: To the praise
issues.canonical: To the praise of the glory of his grace, wherein he hath made us accepted in the beloved.

issues.excerpt: In whom we have redemption through his blood
issues.canonical: In whom we have redemption through his blood, the forgiveness of sins, according to the riches of his grace;

issues.excerpt: Wherein
issues.canonical: Wherein he hath abounded toward us in all wisdom and prudence;

issues.excerpt: Having made known
issues.canonical: Having made known unto us the mystery of his will, according to his good pleasure which he hath purposed in himself:

issues.excerpt: That in the dispensation of the fulness of times he might gather together in one
issues.canonical: That in the dispensation of the fulness of times he might gather together in one all things in Christ, both which are in heaven, and which are on earth; even in him:

issues.excerpt: In whom also we have obtained
issues.canonical: In whom also we have obtained an inheritance, being predestinated according to the purpose of him who worketh all things after the counsel of his own will:

issues.excerpt: That we should be to the praise
issues.canonical: That we should be to the praise of his glory, who first trusted in Christ.

issues.excerpt: Which is the earnest of our inheritance until
issues.canonical: Which is the earnest of our inheritance until the redemption of the purchased possession, unto the praise of his glory.

issues.excerpt: Wherefore I also, after I heard
issues.canonical: Wherefore I also, after I heard of your faith in the Lord Jesus, and love unto all the saints,

issues.excerpt: Cease not to give thanks
issues.canonical: Cease not to give thanks for you, making mention of you in my prayers;

issues.excerpt: That the God
issues.canonical: That the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may give unto you the spirit of wisdom and revelation in the knowledge of him:

issues.excerpt: The eyes
issues.canonical: The eyes of your understanding being enlightened; that ye may know what is the hope of his calling, and what the riches of the glory of his inheritance in the saints,

issues.excerpt: Which he wrought
issues.canonical: Which he wrought in Christ, when he raised him from the dead, and set him at his own right hand in the heavenly places,

issues.excerpt: Far above all principality
issues.canonical: Far above all principality, and power, and might, and dominion, and every name that is named, not only in this world, but also in that which is to come:

issues.excerpt: 23 Which is his <span class="cw" data-tgt="pmillennial_starmillennial_star__millennial_sta
Wherein
issues.canonical: Wherein in time past ye walked according to the course of this world, according to the prince of the power of the air, the spirit that now worketh in the children of disobedience:

issues.excerpt: Among whom also we all had our conversation in times past in the lusts
issues.canonical: Among whom also we all had our conversation in times past in the lusts of our flesh, fulfilling the desires of the flesh and of the mind; and were by nature the children of wrath,

issues.excerpt: But God, who is rich in mercy , for his great
issues.canonical: But God, who is rich in mercy, for his great love wherewith he loved us,

issues.excerpt: Even when we were dead
issues.canonical: Even when we were dead in sins, hath quickened us together with Christ, (by grace ye are saved;)

issues.excerpt: And hath raised
issues.canonical: And hath raised us up together, and made us sit together in heavenly places in Christ Jesus:

issues.excerpt: That in the ages
issues.canonical: That in the ages to come he might shew the exceeding riches of his grace in his kindness toward us through Christ Jesus.

issues.excerpt: For by grace
issues.canonical: For by grace are ye saved through faith; and that not of yourselves: it is the gift of God:

issues.excerpt: Not of works
issues.canonical: Not of works, lest any man should boast.

issues.excerpt: For we are his workmanship
issues.canonical: For we are his workmanship, created in Christ Jesus unto good works, which God hath before ordained that we should walk in them.

issues.excerpt: That at that time ye were without Christ
issues.canonical: That at that time ye were without Christ, being aliens from the commonwealth of Israel, and strangers from the covenants of promise, having no hope, and without God in the world:

issues.excerpt: But now in Christ
issues.canonical: But now in Christ Jesus ye who sometimes were far off are made nigh by the blood of Christ.

issues.excerpt: For he is our peace
issues.canonical: For he is our peace, who hath made both one, and hath broken down the middle wall of partition between us;

issues.excerpt: Having abolished in his flesh
issues.canonical: Having abolished in his flesh the enmity, even the law of commandments contained in ordinances; for to make in himself of twain one new man, so making peace;

issues.excerpt: And that he might reconcile
issues.canonical: And that he might reconcile both unto God in one body by the cross, having slain the enmity thereby:

issues.excerpt: And came and preached
issues.canonical: And came and preached peace to you which were afar off, and to them that were nigh.

issues.excerpt: Now therefore ye are no more strangers
issues.canonical: Now therefore ye are no more strangers and foreigners, but fellowcitizens with the saints, and of the household of God;

issues.excerpt: And are built
issues.canonical: And are built upon the foundation of the apostles and prophets, Jesus Christ himself being the chief corner stone;

issues.excerpt: In whom all the building
issues.canonical: In whom all the building fitly framed together groweth unto an holy temple in the Lord:

issues.excerpt: In whom ye also are builded
issues.canonical: In whom ye also are builded together for an habitation of God through the Spirit.

issues.excerpt: If ye have heard
issues.canonical: If ye have heard of the dispensation of the grace of God which is given me to you-ward:

issues.excerpt: How that by revelation he made known
issues.canonical: How that by revelation he made known unto me the mystery; (as I wrote afore in few words,

issues.excerpt: Whereby, when ye read
issues.canonical: Whereby, when ye read, ye may understand my knowledge in the mystery of Christ)

issues.excerpt: Which in other ages was not made known
issues.canonical: Which in other ages was not made known unto the sons of men, as it is now revealed unto his holy apostles and prophets by the Spirit;

issues.excerpt: That the Gentiles
issues.canonical: That the Gentiles should be fellowheirs, and of the same body, and partakers of his promise in Christ by the gospel:

issues.excerpt: Whereof
issues.canonical: Whereof I was made a minister, according to the gift of the grace of God given unto me by the effectual working of his power.

issues.excerpt: Unto me, who am less
issues.canonical: Unto me, who am less than the least of all saints, is this grace given, that I should preach among the Gentiles the unsearchable riches of Christ;

issues.excerpt: To the intent
issues.canonical: To the intent that now unto the principalities and powers in heavenly places might be known by the church the manifold wisdom of God,

issues.excerpt: According
issues.canonical: According to the eternal purpose which he purposed in Christ Jesus our Lord:

issues.excerpt: In whom we have boldness
issues.canonical: In whom we have boldness and access with confidence by the faith of him.

issues.excerpt: Wherefore I desire
issues.canonical: Wherefore I desire that ye faint not at my tribulations for you, which is your glory.

issues.excerpt: Of whom the whole family in heaven
issues.canonical: Of whom the whole family in heaven and earth is named,

issues.excerpt: That he would grant
issues.canonical: That he would grant you, according to the riches of his glory, to be strengthened with might by his Spirit in the inner man;

issues.excerpt: That Christ
issues.canonical: That Christ may dwell in your hearts by faith; that ye, being rooted and grounded in love,

issues.excerpt: May be able
issues.canonical: May be able to comprehend with all saints what is the breadth, and length, and depth, and height;

issues.excerpt: And to know
issues.canonical: And to know the love of Christ, which passeth knowledge, that ye might be filled with all the fulness of God.

issues.excerpt: Now unto him that is able
issues.canonical: Now unto him that is able to do exceeding abundantly above all that we ask or think, according to the power that worketh in us,

issues.excerpt: I therefore, the prisoner of the Lord , beseech you
issues.canonical: I therefore, the prisoner of the Lord, beseech you that ye walk worthy of the vocation wherewith ye are called,

issues.excerpt: With all lowliness
issues.canonical: With all lowliness and meekness, with longsuffering, forbearing one another in love;

issues.excerpt: Endeavouring to keep the unity
issues.canonical: Endeavouring to keep the unity of the Spirit in the bond of peace.

issues.excerpt: One
issues.canonical: One Lord, one faith, one baptism,

issues.excerpt: One
issues.canonical: One God and Father of all, who is above all, and through all, and in you all.

issues.excerpt: But unto every one
issues.canonical: But unto every one of us is given grace according to the measure of the gift of Christ.

issues.excerpt: Wherefore he saith, When he ascended
issues.canonical: Wherefore he saith, When he ascended up on high, he led captivity captive, and gave gifts unto men.

issues.excerpt: (Now that he ascended
issues.canonical: (Now that he ascended, what is it but that he also descended first into the lower parts of the earth?

issues.excerpt: He that descended
issues.canonical: He that descended is the same also that ascended up far above all heavens, that he might fill all things.)

issues.excerpt: And he gave some, apostles
issues.canonical: And he gave some, apostles; and some, prophets; and some, evangelists; and some, pastors and teachers;

issues.excerpt: For the perfecting
issues.canonical: For the perfecting of the saints, for the work of the ministry, for the edifying of the body of Christ:

issues.excerpt: Till we all come in the unity
issues.canonical: Till we all come in the unity of the faith, and of the knowledge of the Son of God, unto a perfect man, unto the measure of the stature of the fulness of Christ:

issues.excerpt: But speaking
issues.canonical: But speaking the truth in love, may grow up into him in all things, which is the head, even Christ:

issues.excerpt: From whom the whole
issues.canonical: From whom the whole body fitly joined together and compacted by that which every joint supplieth, according to the effectual working in the measure of every part, maketh increase o

issues.excerpt: This I say
issues.canonical: This I say therefore, and testify in the Lord, that ye henceforth walk not as other Gentiles walk, in the vanity of their mind,

issues.excerpt: Having the understanding
issues.canonical: Having the understanding darkened, being alienated from the life of God through the ignorance that is in them, because of the blindness of their heart:

issues.excerpt: Who being past
issues.canonical: Who being past feeling have given themselves over unto lasciviousness, to work all uncleanness with greediness.

issues.excerpt: But ye have not so learned
issues.canonical: But ye have not so learned Christ;

issues.excerpt: If so be that ye have heard him, and have been taught
issues.canonical: If so be that ye have heard him, and have been taught by him, as the truth is in Jesus:

issues.excerpt: That ye put off
issues.canonical: That ye put off concerning the former conversation the old man, which is corrupt according to the deceitful lusts;

issues.excerpt: And that ye put on the new
issues.canonical: And that ye put on the new man, which after God is created in righteousness and true holiness.

issues.excerpt: Wherefore putting
issues.canonical: Wherefore putting away lying, speak every man truth with his neighbour: for we are members one of another.

issues.excerpt: Let him that stole
issues.canonical: Let him that stole steal no more: but rather let him labour, working with his hands the thing which is good, that he may have to give to him that needeth.

issues.excerpt: Let no corrupt
issues.canonical: Let no corrupt communication proceed out of your mouth, but that which is good to the use of edifying, that it may minister grace unto the hearers.

issues.excerpt: And grieve not the holy
issues.canonical: And grieve not the holy Spirit of God, whereby ye are sealed unto the day of redemption.

issues.excerpt: Let all bitterness
issues.canonical: Let all bitterness, and wrath, and anger, and clamour, and evil speaking, be put away from you, with all malice:

issues.excerpt: And be ye kind one
issues.canonical: And be ye kind one to another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another, even as God for Christ’s sake hath forgiven you.

issues.excerpt: And walk in love
issues.canonical: And walk in love, as Christ also hath loved us, and hath given himself for us an offering and a sacrifice to God for a sweetsmelling savour.

issues.excerpt: But fornication , and all uncleanness
issues.canonical: But fornication, and all uncleanness, or covetousness, let it not be once named among you, as becometh saints;

issues.excerpt: Neither filthiness
issues.canonical: Neither filthiness, nor foolish talking, nor jesting, which are not convenient: but rather giving of thanks.

issues.excerpt: For this ye know
issues.canonical: For this ye know, that no whoremonger, nor unclean person, nor covetous man, who is an idolater, hath any inheritance in the kingdom of Christ and of God.

issues.excerpt: Let no man deceive you with vain words: for because of these things cometh
issues.canonical: Let no man deceive you with vain words: for because of these things cometh the wrath of God upon the children of disobedience.

issues.excerpt: 7 Be not ye therefore <span class="cw" data-tgt="pjstjst__inspired_version_18670.458"><
For ye were sometimes
issues.canonical: For ye were sometimes darkness, but now are ye light in the Lord: walk as children of light:

issues.excerpt: (For the fruit
issues.canonical: (For the fruit of the Spirit is in all goodness and righteousness and truth;)

issues.excerpt: Proving what is acceptable
issues.canonical: Proving what is acceptable unto the Lord.

issues.excerpt: And have no fellowship
issues.canonical: And have no fellowship with the unfruitful works of darkness, but rather reprove them.

issues.excerpt: For it is a shame
issues.canonical: For it is a shame even to speak of those things which are done of them in secret.

issues.excerpt: But all things that are reproved
issues.canonical: But all things that are reproved are made manifest by the light: for whatsoever doth make manifest is light.

issues.excerpt: Wherefore he saith, Awake
issues.canonical: Wherefore he saith, Awake thou that sleepest, and arise from the dead, and Christ shall give thee light.

issues.excerpt: Wherefore be ye not unwise
issues.canonical: Wherefore be ye not unwise, but understanding what the will of the Lord is.

issues.excerpt: And be not drunk
issues.canonical: And be not drunk with wine, wherein is excess; but be filled with the Spirit;

issues.excerpt: Speaking to yourselves in psalms
issues.canonical: Speaking to yourselves in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs, singing and making melody in your heart to the Lord;

issues.excerpt: Giving thanks always for all things unto God
issues.canonical: Giving thanks always for all things unto God and the Father in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ;

issues.excerpt: Submitting
issues.canonical: Submitting yourselves one to another in the fear of God.

issues.excerpt: Wives
issues.canonical: Wives, submit yourselves unto your own husbands, as unto the Lord.

issues.excerpt: For the husband
issues.canonical: For the husband is the head of the wife, even as Christ is the head of the church: and he is the saviour of the body.

issues.excerpt: Therefore as the church
issues.canonical: Therefore as the church is subject unto Christ, so let the wives be to their own husbands in every thing.

issues.excerpt: Husbands
issues.canonical: Husbands, love your wives, even as Christ also loved the church, and gave himself for it;

issues.excerpt: That he might sanctify
issues.canonical: That he might sanctify and cleanse it with the washing of water by the word,

issues.excerpt: That he might present
issues.canonical: That he might present it to himself a glorious church, not having spot, or wrinkle, or any such thing; but that it should be holy and without blemish.

issues.excerpt: So ought
issues.canonical: So ought men to love their wives as their own bodies. He that loveth his wife loveth himself.

issues.excerpt: For no man ever
issues.canonical: For no man ever yet hated his own flesh; but nourisheth and cherisheth it, even as the Lord the church:

issues.excerpt: For we are members
issues.canonical: For we are members of his body, of his flesh, and of his bones.

issues.excerpt: For this cause shall a man leave
issues.canonical: For this cause shall a man leave his father and mother, and shall be joined unto his wife, and they two shall be one flesh.

issues.excerpt: This is a great
issues.canonical: This is a great mystery: but I speak concerning Christ and the church.

issues.excerpt: Nevertheless let every one
issues.canonical: Nevertheless let every one of you in particular so love his wife even as himself; and the wife see that she reverence her husband.

issues.excerpt: Honour
issues.canonical: Honour thy father and mother; (which is the first commandment with promise;)

issues.excerpt: That it may
issues.canonical: That it may be well with thee, and thou mayest live long on the earth.

issues.excerpt: And, ye fathers
issues.canonical: And, ye fathers, provoke not your children to wrath: but bring them up in the nurture and admonition of the Lord.

issues.excerpt: Servants
issues.canonical: Servants, be obedient to them that are your masters according to the flesh, with fear and trembling, in singleness of your heart, as unto Christ;

issues.excerpt: Not with eyeservice , as menpleasers
issues.canonical: Not with eyeservice, as menpleasers; but as the servants of Christ, doing the will of God from the heart;

issues.excerpt: With good will doing service
issues.canonical: With good will doing service, as to the Lord, and not to men:

issues.excerpt: Knowing that whatsoever good
issues.canonical: Knowing that whatsoever good thing any man doeth, the same shall he receive of the Lord, whether he be bond or free.

issues.excerpt: And, ye masters , do the same things unto them, forbearing
issues.canonical: And, ye masters, do the same things unto them, forbearing threatening: knowing that your Master also is in heaven; neither is there respect of persons with him.

issues.excerpt: Finally
issues.canonical: Finally, my brethren, be strong in the Lord, and in the power of his might.

issues.excerpt: Put on the whole
issues.canonical: Put on the whole armour of God, that ye may be able to stand against the wiles of the devil.

issues.excerpt: For we wrestle not against flesh and blood , but against principalities , against powers
issues.canonical: For we wrestle not against flesh and blood, but against principalities, against powers, against the rulers of the darkness of this world, against spiritual wickedness in high place

issues.excerpt: Wherefore take unto you
issues.canonical: Wherefore take unto you the whole armour of God, that ye may be able to withstand in the evil day, and having done all, to stand.

issues.excerpt: Stand
issues.canonical: Stand therefore, having your loins girt about with truth, and having on the breastplate of righteousness;

issues.excerpt: And your feet
issues.canonical: And your feet shod with the preparation of the gospel of peace;

issues.excerpt: Above all, taking the shield
issues.canonical: Above all, taking the shield of faith, wherewith ye shall be able to quench all the fiery darts of the wicked.

issues.excerpt: And take the helmet
issues.canonical: And take the helmet of salvation, and the sword of the Spirit, which is the word of God:

issues.excerpt: Praying always with all prayer and supplication in the Spirit
issues.canonical: Praying always with all prayer and supplication in the Spirit, and watching thereunto with all perseverance and supplication for all saints;

issues.excerpt: And for me, that utterance
issues.canonical: And for me, that utterance may be given unto me, that I may open my mouth boldly, to make known the mystery of the gospel,

issues.excerpt: Whom I have sent unto you
issues.canonical: Whom I have sent unto you for the same purpose, that ye might know our affairs, and that he might comfort your hearts.

issues.excerpt: Grace
issues.canonical: Grace be with all them that love our Lord Jesus Christ in sincerity. Amen.

And I take mine account from the twenty
issues.canonical: And I take mine account from the twenty and four plates which were found by the people of Limhi, which is called the Book of Ether.

issues.excerpt: And as I suppose
issues.canonical: And as I suppose that the first part of this record, which speaks concerning the creation of the world, and also of Adam, and an account from that time even to the great tower, and

issues.excerpt: Coriantor
issues.canonical: Coriantor was the son of Moron.

issues.excerpt: And Moron
issues.canonical: And Moron was the son of Ethem.

issues.excerpt: And Ethem
issues.canonical: And Ethem was the son of Ahah.

issues.excerpt: And Ahah
issues.canonical: And Ahah was the son of Seth.

issues.excerpt: And Seth
issues.canonical: And Seth was the son of Shiblon.

issues.excerpt: And Shiblon
issues.canonical: And Shiblon was the son of Com.

issues.excerpt: And Com
issues.canonical: And Com was the son of Coriantum.

issues.excerpt: And Coriantum
issues.canonical: And Coriantum was the son of Amnigaddah.

issues.excerpt: And Aaron
issues.canonical: And Aaron was a descendant of Heth, who was the son of Hearthom.

issues.excerpt: And Kish
issues.canonical: And Kish was the son of Corom.

issues.excerpt: And Corom
issues.canonical: And Corom was the son of Levi.

issues.excerpt: And Levi
issues.canonical: And Levi was the son of Kim.

issues.excerpt: And Kim
issues.canonical: And Kim was the son of Morianton.

issues.excerpt: And Riplakish was the son
issues.canonical: And Riplakish was the son of Shez.

issues.excerpt: And Shez
issues.canonical: And Shez was the son of Heth.

issues.excerpt: And Heth
issues.canonical: And Heth was the son of Com.

issues.excerpt: And Com
issues.canonical: And Com was the son of Coriantum.

issues.excerpt: And Coriantum
issues.canonical: And Coriantum was the son of Emer.

issues.excerpt: And Shule
issues.canonical: And Shule was the son of Kib.

issues.canonical: And after that he had established himself king he did ease the burden of the people, by which he did gain favor in the eyes of the people, and they did anoint him to be their king.

issues.excerpt: And it came to pass
issues.canonical: And it came to pass that Kish passed away also, and Lib reigned in his stead.

issues.excerpt: And they did preserve
issues.canonical: And they did preserve the land southward for a wilderness, to get game. And the whole face of the land northward was covered with inhabitants.

issues.excerpt: And they were exceedingly industrious , and they did buy
issues.canonical: And they were exceedingly industrious, and they did buy and sell and traffic one with another, that they might get gain.

issues.excerpt: And they did work

issues.excerpt: And it came to pass that Lib did live many years
issues.canonical: And it came to pass that Lib did live many years, and begat sons and daughters; and he also begat Hearthom.

issues.excerpt: Now Com did fight
issues.canonical: Now Com did fight against them much; nevertheless, he did not prevail against them.

issues.excerpt: And they prophesied
issues.canonical: And they prophesied unto Com many things; and he was blessed in all the remainder of his days.

issues.excerpt: And they hearkened
issues.canonical: And they hearkened not unto the voice of the Lord, because of their wicked combinations; wherefore, there began to be wars and contentions in all the land, and also many famines an

issues.excerpt: And it came to pass that Shiblom was slain, and Seth was brought into captivity , and did dwell
issues.canonical: And it came to pass that Shiblom was slain, and Seth was brought into captivity, and did dwell in captivity all his days.

issues.canonical: And it came to pass that Ethem did execute judgment in wickedness all his days; and he begat Moron. And it came to pass that Moron did reign in his stead; and Moron did that which

issues.canonical: And it came to pass that there arose a rebellion among the people, because of that secret combination which was built up to get power and gain; and there arose a mighty man among t

issues.excerpt: And it came to pass
issues.canonical: And it came to pass that Moron did overthrow him, and did obtain the kingdom again.

issues.excerpt: And it came to pass
issues.canonical: And it came to pass that there arose another mighty man; and he was a descendant of the brother of Jared.

issues.excerpt: And it came to pass
issues.canonical: And it came to pass that he did overthrow Moron and obtain the kingdom; wherefore, Moron dwelt in captivity all the remainder of his days; and he begat Coriantor.

issues.excerpt: For he did cry

issues.excerpt: Wherefore, whoso believeth in God might with surety hope

issues.excerpt: And now, I, Moroni , would speak somewhat concerning these things; I would show unto the world that faith
issues.canonical: And now, I, Moroni, would speak somewhat concerning these things; I would show unto the world that faith is things which are hoped for and not seen; wherefore, dispute not because

issues.excerpt: Wherefore, by faith was the law of Moses given. But in the gift
issues.canonical: Wherefore, by faith was the law of Moses given. But in the gift of his Son hath God prepared a more excellent way; and it is by faith that it hath been fulfilled.

issues.excerpt: For if there be no faith
issues.canonical: For if there be no faith among the children of men God can do no miracle among them; wherefore, he showed not himself until after their faith.

issues.excerpt: Behold, it was the faith of Ammon
issues.canonical: Behold, it was the faith of Ammon and his brethren which wrought so great a miracle among the Lamanites.

issues.excerpt: Yea, and even all they who wrought
issues.canonical: Yea, and even all they who wrought miracles wrought them by faith, even those who were before Christ and also those who were after.

issues.excerpt: And it was by faith that the three disciples obtained
issues.canonical: And it was by faith that the three disciples obtained a promise that they should not taste of death; and they obtained not the promise until after their faith.

issues.excerpt: And neither at any time hath any wrought
issues.canonical: And neither at any time hath any wrought miracles until after their faith; wherefore they first believed in the Son of God.

issues.excerpt: And there were many whose faith
issues.canonical: And there were many whose faith was so exceedingly strong, even before Christ came, who could not be kept from within the veil, but truly saw with their eyes the things which they

issues.excerpt: And I said unto him: Lord
issues.canonical: And I said unto him: Lord, the Gentiles will mock at these things, because of our weakness in writing; for Lord thou hast made us mighty in word by faith, but thou hast not made us

issues.excerpt: Thou hast also made our words powerful and great
issues.canonical: Thou hast also made our words powerful and great, even that we cannot write them; wherefore, when we write we behold our weakness, and stumble because of the placing of our words;

issues.excerpt: And if men come unto me I will show unto them their weakness . I give unto men weakness that they may be humble
issues.canonical: And if men come unto me I will show unto them their weakness. I give unto men weakness that they may be humble; and my grace is sufficient for all men that humble themselves before

issues.excerpt: Behold, I will show unto the Gentiles their weakness , and I will show unto them that faith
issues.canonical: Behold, I will show unto the Gentiles their weakness, and I will show unto them that faith, hope and charity bringeth unto me—the fountain of all righteousness.

issues.excerpt: For thus didst thou manifest thyself
issues.canonical: For thus didst thou manifest thyself unto thy disciples; for after they had faith, and did speak in thy name, thou didst show thyself unto them in great power.

issues.excerpt: And I also remember that thou hast said that thou hast prepared
issues.canonical: And I also remember that thou hast said that thou hast prepared a house for man, yea, even among the mansions of thy Father, in which man might have a more excellent hope; wherefor

issues.excerpt: And now I know that this love which thou hast had for the children of men is charity ; wherefore, except men shall have charity they cannot
issues.canonical: And now I know that this love which thou hast had for the children of men is charity; wherefore, except men shall have charity they cannot inherit that place which thou hast prepar

issues.excerpt: And it came to pass
issues.canonical: And it came to pass that I prayed unto the Lord that he would give unto the Gentiles grace, that they might have charity.

issues.excerpt: And it came to pass that the Lord
issues.canonical: And it came to pass that the Lord said unto me: If they have not charity it mattereth not unto thee, thou hast been faithful; wherefore, thy garments shall be made clean. And becau

issues.canonical: And now, I would commend you to seek this Jesus of whom the prophets and apostles have written, that the grace of God the Father, and also the Lord Jesus Christ, and the Holy Ghost

issues.excerpt: And that it was the place of the New
issues.canonical: And that it was the place of the New Jerusalem, which should come down out of heaven, and the holy sanctuary of the Lord.

issues.excerpt: Behold
issues.canonical: Behold, Ether saw the days of Christ, and he spake concerning a New Jerusalem upon this land.

issues.excerpt: And he spake
issues.canonical: And he spake also concerning the house of Israel, and the Jerusalem from whence Lehi should come—after it should be destroyed it should be built up again, a holy city unto the Lord

issues.excerpt: And that a New

issues.canonical: And I commanded, and search hath been made, and it is found that this city of old time hath made insurrection against kings, and that rebellion and sedition have been made therein.

issues.excerpt: Who gave
issues.canonical: Who gave himself for our sins, that he might deliver us from this present evil world, according to the will of God and our Father:

issues.excerpt: I marvel
issues.canonical: I marvel that ye are so soon removed from him that called you into the grace of Christ unto another gospel:

issues.excerpt: Which is not another; but there be some that trouble
issues.canonical: Which is not another; but there be some that trouble you, and would pervert the gospel of Christ.

issues.excerpt: But though we, or an angel from heaven
issues.canonical: But though we, or an angel from heaven, preach any other gospel unto you than that which we have preached unto you, let him be accursed.

issues.excerpt: As we said before, so say
issues.canonical: As we said before, so say I now again, If any man preach any other gospel unto you than that ye have received, let him be accursed.

issues.excerpt: For do I now persuade men , or God
issues.canonical: For do I now persuade men, or God? or do I seek to please men? for if I yet pleased men, I should not be the servant of Christ.

issues.excerpt: But I certify you
issues.canonical: But I certify you, brethren, that the gospel which was preached of me is not after man.

issues.excerpt: For I neither received
issues.canonical: For I neither received it of man, neither was I taught it, but by the revelation of Jesus Christ.

issues.excerpt: For ye have heard
issues.canonical: For ye have heard of my conversation in time past in the Jews’ religion, how that beyond measure I persecuted the church of God, and wasted it:

issues.excerpt: But when it pleased
issues.canonical: But when it pleased God, who separated me from my mother’s womb, and called me by his grace,

issues.excerpt: To reveal his Son
issues.canonical: To reveal his Son in me, that I might preach him among the heathen; immediately I conferred not with flesh and blood:

issues.excerpt: Neither went I up to Jerusalem to them which were apostles
issues.canonical: Neither went I up to Jerusalem to them which were apostles before me; but I went into Arabia, and returned again unto Damascus.

issues.excerpt: Then after three
issues.canonical: Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to see Peter, and abode with him fifteen days.

issues.excerpt: But other of the apostles
issues.canonical: But other of the apostles saw I none, save James the Lord’s brother.

issues.excerpt: Now the things which I write
issues.canonical: Now the things which I write unto you, behold, before God, I lie not.

issues.excerpt: But they had heard
issues.canonical: But they had heard only, That he which persecuted us in times past now preacheth the faith which once he destroyed.

issues.excerpt: And they glorified
issues.canonical: And they glorified God in me.

issues.excerpt: And I went up by revelation , and communicated
issues.canonical: And I went up by revelation, and communicated unto them that gospel which I preach among the Gentiles, but privately to them which were of reputation, lest by any means I should ru

issues.excerpt: But neither Titus
issues.canonical: But neither Titus, who was with me, being a Greek, was compelled to be circumcised:

issues.excerpt: And that because of false
issues.canonical: And that because of false brethren unawares brought in, who came in privily to spy out our liberty which we have in Christ Jesus, that they might bring us into bondage:

issues.excerpt: But of these who seemed
issues.canonical: But of these who seemed to be somewhat, (whatsoever they were, it maketh no matter to me: God accepteth no man’s person:) for they who seemed to be somewhat in conference added not

issues.excerpt: (For he that wrought
issues.canonical: (For he that wrought effectually in Peter to the apostleship of the circumcision, the same was mighty in me toward the Gentiles:)

issues.excerpt: And when James , Cephas, and John
issues.canonical: And when James, Cephas, and John, who seemed to be pillars, perceived the grace that was given unto me, they gave to me and Barnabas the right hands of fellowship; that we should g

issues.excerpt: But when Peter was come to Antioch
issues.canonical: But when Peter was come to Antioch, I withstood him to the face, because he was to be blamed.

issues.excerpt: For before that certain
issues.canonical: For before that certain came from James, he did eat with the Gentiles: but when they were come, he withdrew and separated himself, fearing them which were of the circumcision.

issues.excerpt: And the other Jews
issues.canonical: And the other Jews dissembled likewise with him; insomuch that Barnabas also was carried away with their dissimulation.

issues.excerpt: But when I saw that they walked not uprightly according to the truth of the gospel , I said unto Peter before [them] all, If thou, being a Jew
issues.canonical: But when I saw that they walked not uprightly according to the truth of the gospel, I said unto Peter before them all, If thou, being a Jew, livest after the manner of Gentiles, an

issues.excerpt: Knowing that a man is not justified
issues.canonical: Knowing that a man is not justified by the works of the law, but by the faith of Jesus Christ, even we have believed in Jesus Christ, that we might be justified by the faith of Chr

issues.excerpt: But if, while
issues.canonical: But if, while we seek to be justified by Christ, we ourselves also are found sinners, is therefore Christ the minister of sin? God forbid.

issues.excerpt: For if I build
issues.canonical: For if I build again the things which I destroyed, I make myself a transgressor.

issues.excerpt: I am crucified
issues.canonical: I am crucified with Christ: nevertheless I live; yet not I, but Christ liveth in me: and the life which I now live in the flesh I live by the faith of the Son of God, who loved me,

issues.excerpt: I do not frustrate the grace of God
issues.canonical: I do not frustrate the grace of God: for if righteousness come by the law, then Christ is dead in vain.

issues.excerpt: O foolish Galatians, who hath bewitched
issues.canonical: O foolish Galatians, who hath bewitched you, that ye should not obey the truth, before whose eyes Jesus Christ hath been evidently set forth, crucified among you?

issues.excerpt: This only would I learn of you
issues.canonical: This only would I learn of you, Received ye the Spirit by the works of the law, or by the hearing of faith?

issues.excerpt: Are ye so foolish ? having begun
issues.canonical: Are ye so foolish? having begun in the Spirit, are ye now made perfect by the flesh?

issues.excerpt: He therefore that ministereth to you
issues.canonical: He therefore that ministereth to you the Spirit, and worketh miracles among you, doeth he it by the works of the law, or by the hearing of faith?

issues.excerpt: Even as Abraham
issues.canonical: Even as Abraham believed God, and it was accounted to him for righteousness.

issues.excerpt: Know
issues.canonical: Know ye therefore that they which are of faith, the same are the children of Abraham.

issues.excerpt: And the scripture , foreseeing
issues.canonical: And the scripture, foreseeing that God would justify the heathen through faith, preached before the gospel unto Abraham, saying, In thee shall all nations be blessed.

issues.excerpt: So then they which be of faith
issues.canonical: So then they which be of faith are blessed with faithful Abraham.

issues.excerpt: For as many as are of the works of the law
issues.canonical: For as many as are of the works of the law are under the curse: for it is written, Cursed is every one that continueth not in all things which are written in the book of the law to

issues.excerpt: But that no man is justified
issues.canonical: But that no man is justified by the law in the sight of God, it is evident: for, The just shall live by faith.

issues.excerpt: And the law
issues.canonical: And the law is not of faith: but, The man that doeth them shall live in them.

issues.excerpt: Christ hath redeemed us from the curse
issues.canonical: Christ hath redeemed us from the curse of the law, being made a curse for us: for it is written, Cursed is every one that hangeth on a tree:

issues.excerpt: That the blessing
issues.canonical: That the blessing of Abraham might come on the Gentiles through Jesus Christ; that we might receive the promise of the Spirit through faith.

issues.excerpt: Now to Abraham
issues.canonical: Now to Abraham and his seed were the promises made. He saith not, And to seeds, as of many; but as of one, And to thy seed, which is Christ.

issues.excerpt: And this I say
issues.canonical: And this I say, that the covenant, that was confirmed before of God in Christ, the law, which was four hundred and thirty years after, cannot disannul, that it should make the prom

issues.excerpt: For if the inheritance
issues.canonical: For if the inheritance be of the law, it is no more of promise: but God gave it to Abraham by promise.

issues.excerpt: Now a mediator
issues.canonical: Now a mediator is not a mediator of one, but God is one.

issues.excerpt: But the scripture
issues.canonical: But the scripture hath concluded all under sin, that the promise by faith of Jesus Christ might be given to them that believe.

issues.excerpt: But before faith
issues.canonical: But before faith came, we were kept under the law, shut up unto the faith which should afterwards be revealed.

issues.excerpt: Wherefore the law
issues.canonical: Wherefore the law was our schoolmaster to bring us unto Christ, that we might be justified by faith.

issues.excerpt: But after that faith
issues.canonical: But after that faith is come, we are no longer under a schoolmaster.

issues.excerpt: For ye are all the children
issues.canonical: For ye are all the children of God by faith in Christ Jesus.

issues.excerpt: For as many of you
issues.canonical: For as many of you as have been baptized into Christ have put on Christ.

issues.excerpt: There is neither Jew nor Greek , there is neither bond
issues.canonical: There is neither Jew nor Greek, there is neither bond nor free, there is neither male nor female: for ye are all one in Christ Jesus.

issues.excerpt: Even so we, when we were children
issues.canonical: Even so we, when we were children, were in bondage under the elements of the world:

issues.excerpt: But when the fulness
issues.canonical: But when the fulness of the time was come, God sent forth his Son, made of a woman, made under the law,

issues.excerpt: To redeem
issues.canonical: To redeem them that were under the law, that we might receive the adoption of sons.

issues.excerpt: And because ye are sons
issues.canonical: And because ye are sons, God hath sent forth the Spirit of his Son into your hearts, crying, Abba, Father.

issues.excerpt: Wherefore thou art no more a servant
issues.canonical: Wherefore thou art no more a servant, but a son; and if a son, then an heir of God through Christ.

issues.excerpt: Howbeit
issues.canonical: Howbeit then, when ye knew not God, ye did service unto them which by nature are no gods.

issues.excerpt: But now, after that ye have known
issues.canonical: But now, after that ye have known God, or rather are known of God, how turn ye again to the weak and beggarly elements, whereunto ye desire again to be in bondage?

issues.excerpt: Ye observe
issues.canonical: Ye observe days, and months, and times, and years.

issues.excerpt: I am afraid of you
issues.canonical: I am afraid of you, lest I have bestowed upon you labour in vain.

issues.excerpt: Brethren
issues.canonical: Brethren, I beseech you, be as I am; for I am as ye are: ye have not injured me at all.

issues.excerpt: Ye know
issues.canonical: Ye know how through infirmity of the flesh I preached the gospel unto you at the first.

issues.excerpt: And my temptation which was in my flesh
issues.canonical: And my temptation which was in my flesh ye despised not, nor rejected; but received me as an angel of God, even as Christ Jesus.

issues.excerpt: Where is then the blessedness
issues.canonical: Where is then the blessedness ye spake of? for I bear you record, that, if it had been possible, ye would have plucked out your own eyes, and have given them to me.

issues.excerpt: They zealously
issues.canonical: They zealously affect you, but not well; yea, they would exclude you, that ye might affect them.

issues.excerpt: My little children
issues.canonical: My little children, of whom I travail in birth again until Christ be formed in you,

issues.excerpt: I desire
issues.canonical: I desire to be present with you now, and to change my voice; for I stand in doubt of you.

issues.excerpt: Tell me, ye that desire to be under the law
issues.canonical: Tell me, ye that desire to be under the law, do ye not hear the law?

issues.excerpt: For it is written
issues.canonical: For it is written, that Abraham had two sons, the one by a bondmaid, the other by a freewoman.

issues.excerpt: Which things are an allegory: for these are the two
issues.canonical: Which things are an allegory: for these are the two covenants; the one from the mount Sinai, which gendereth to bondage, which is Agar.

issues.excerpt: For this Agar
issues.canonical: For this Agar is mount Sinai in Arabia, and answereth to Jerusalem which now is, and is in bondage with her children.

issues.excerpt: For it is written
issues.canonical: For it is written, Rejoice, thou barren that bearest not; break forth and cry, thou that travailest not: for the desolate hath many more children than she which hath an husband.

issues.excerpt: But as then he that was born
issues.canonical: But as then he that was born after the flesh persecuted him that was born after the Spirit, even so it is now.

issues.excerpt: Nevertheless
issues.canonical: Nevertheless what saith the scripture? Cast out the bondwoman and her son: for the son of the bondwoman shall not be heir with the son of the freewoman.

issues.excerpt: For I testify
issues.canonical: For I testify again to every man that is circumcised, that he is a debtor to do the whole law.

issues.excerpt: Christ
issues.canonical: Christ is become of no effect unto you, whosoever of you are justified by the law; ye are fallen from grace.

issues.excerpt: For in Jesus
issues.canonical: For in Jesus Christ neither circumcision availeth any thing, nor uncircumcision; but faith which worketh by love.

issues.excerpt: Ye did run
issues.canonical: Ye did run well; who did hinder you that ye should not obey the truth?

issues.excerpt: A little leaven
issues.canonical: A little leaven leaveneth the whole lump.

issues.excerpt: I have confidence
issues.canonical: I have confidence in you through the Lord, that ye will be none otherwise minded: but he that troubleth you shall bear his judgment, whosoever he be.

issues.excerpt: And I, brethren
issues.canonical: And I, brethren, if I yet preach circumcision, why do I yet suffer persecution? then is the offence of the cross ceased.

issues.excerpt: I would they were even cut
issues.canonical: I would they were even cut off which trouble you.

issues.excerpt: For, brethren
issues.canonical: For, brethren, ye have been called unto liberty; only use not liberty for an occasion to the flesh, but by love serve one another.

issues.excerpt: For all the law is fulfilled in one
issues.canonical: For all the law is fulfilled in one word, even in this; Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.

issues.excerpt: But if ye bite
issues.canonical: But if ye bite and devour one another, take heed that ye be not consumed one of another.

issues.excerpt: For the flesh
issues.canonical: For the flesh lusteth against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh: and these are contrary the one to the other: so that ye cannot do the things that ye would.

issues.excerpt: But if ye be led
issues.canonical: But if ye be led of the Spirit, ye are not under the law.

issues.excerpt: Now the works
issues.canonical: Now the works of the flesh are manifest, which are these; Adultery, fornication, uncleanness, lasciviousness,

issues.excerpt: Idolatry
issues.canonical: Idolatry, witchcraft, hatred, variance, emulations, wrath, strife, seditions, heresies,

issues.excerpt: Envyings
issues.canonical: Envyings, murders, drunkenness, revellings, and such like: of the which I tell you before, as I have also told you in time past, that they which do such things shall not inherit th

issues.excerpt: But the fruit
issues.canonical: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, longsuffering, gentleness, goodness, faith,

issues.excerpt: Let us not be desirous of vain
issues.canonical: Let us not be desirous of vain glory, provoking one another, envying one another.

issues.excerpt: Bear
issues.canonical: Bear ye one another’s burdens, and so fulfil the law of Christ.

issues.excerpt: For if a man think
issues.canonical: For if a man think himself to be something, when he is nothing, he deceiveth himself.

issues.excerpt: But let every man prove
issues.canonical: But let every man prove his own work, and then shall he have rejoicing in himself alone, and not in another.

issues.excerpt: For every man shall bear
issues.canonical: For every man shall bear his own burden.

issues.excerpt: Be not deceived ; God
issues.canonical: Be not deceived; God is not mocked: for whatsoever a man soweth, that shall he also reap.

issues.excerpt: For he that soweth
issues.canonical: For he that soweth to his flesh shall of the flesh reap corruption; but he that soweth to the Spirit shall of the Spirit reap life everlasting.

issues.excerpt: And let us not be weary
issues.canonical: And let us not be weary in well doing: for in due season we shall reap, if we faint not.

issues.excerpt: As we have therefore opportunity , let us do good
issues.canonical: As we have therefore opportunity, let us do good unto all men, especially unto them who are of the household of faith.

issues.excerpt: As many as desire to make a fair shew in the flesh , they constrain you
issues.canonical: As many as desire to make a fair shew in the flesh, they constrain you to be circumcised; only lest they should suffer persecution for the cross of Christ.

issues.excerpt: For neither they themselves who are circumcised
issues.canonical: For neither they themselves who are circumcised keep the law; but desire to have you circumcised, that they may glory in your flesh.

issues.excerpt: But God
issues.canonical: But God forbid that I should glory, save in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom the world is crucified unto me, and I unto the world.

issues.excerpt: For in Christ
issues.canonical: For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision availeth any thing, nor uncircumcision, but a new creature.

issues.excerpt: And as many as walk
issues.canonical: And as many as walk according to this rule, peace be on them, and mercy, and upon the Israel of God.

issues.excerpt: From henceforth
issues.canonical: From henceforth let no man trouble me: for I bear in my body the marks of the Lord Jesus.

issues.excerpt: Brethren , the grace
issues.canonical: Brethren, the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.

issues.excerpt: Women received their dead raised to life again: and others were tortured , not accepting
issues.canonical: Women received their dead raised to life again: and others were tortured, not accepting deliverance; that they might obtain a better resurrection:

issues.excerpt: And for this cause he is the mediator of the new testament , that by means of death , for the redemption of the transgressions [that were] under the first testament, they which are
issues.canonical: And for this cause he is the mediator of the new testament, that by means of death, for the redemption of the transgressions that were under the first testament, they which are cal

issues.excerpt: Yea, how quick to be lifted up in pride; yea, how quick to boast, and do all manner of that
issues.canonical: Yea, how quick to be lifted up in pride; yea, how quick to boast, and do all manner of that which is iniquity; and how slow are they to remember the Lord their God, and to give ear

issues.excerpt: And I would that ye should behold that the more part of them are in the path of their duty, and they do walk circumspectly before God, and they do observe to keep his commandments
issues.canonical: And I would that ye should behold that the more part of them are in the path of their duty, and they do walk circumspectly before God, and they do observe to keep his commandments
